Against odds, Auburn Smith, become nationally ranked wheelchair tennis player.
will participate USTA-ITF Junior Wheelchair Tennis Camp.
PORT ORANGE Auburn Smith born into sports.
also born with spina bifida, most common permanently disabling birth defect United States, according Spina Bifida Association.
Literally meaning "split spine" Latin, spina bifida occurs when baby womb spinal column does close way.
Across United States, roughly eight babies born with spina bifida similar defect brain spine every day.
Against odds, Smith, become nationally ranked wheelchair tennis player.
will participate USTA-ITF Junior Wheelchair Tennis Camp Mission Viejo, Calif. from July 28-Aug.
athletes from ages 12-18 from across globe, spanning states countries, will attend camp improve their skills receive instruction from some coaches world.
This will Smith family's fifth straight summer trip Southern California.
Smith primarily enjoys getting friends made over last five years.
campers spend virtually entire six-day period together, including annual trip theme park.
James, USTA's national head coach national manager wheelchair tennis, will primary instructors camp.
Even though coached four Paralympic games, says camp favorite week year.
soon-to-be junior Spruce Creek began playing tennis
Since then, worked with Nancy Olson, runs "Tennis Everyone!"
program Easter Seals Volusia Flagler Counties.
Olson helped Smith gain exposure across country since, opinion, Florida recognized wheelchair tennis hotbed.
wanted have example good could really good tennis," said Olson, two-time Paralympic silver medalist wheelchair tennis.
Smith named National Youth Representative Easter Seals Oct. 2012, becoming first national representative from Easter Seals affiliate Volusia Flagler counties.

Born Jessamine County, Kentucky, Butler studied after graduating from Transylvania University.
served 1812, taking part Battle Thames Battle Orleans.
After war, practiced Carrollton, Kentucky briefly served Kentucky House Representatives.
served United States House Representatives from 1839 1843 before running Governor Kentucky 1844.
lost 1844 gubernatorial election Whig nominee William Owsley.
During Mexican-American War, Butler served major general volunteers.
General Zachary Taylor's second-in-command during Battle Monterrey later succeeded Winfield Scott commander American forces occupying Mexico City.
1848 Democratic National Convention nominated ticket Cass Butler, Whig ticket Taylor Millard Fillmore 1848 presidential election.
attended Peace Conference 1861, which sought defuse secession crisis that arose following 1860 presidential election.
During Civil War, Butler Democrat favored Union.
Butler born Jessamine County, Kentucky (then Fayette County), graduated from Transylvania University 1812.
began study with Robert Wickliffe, education interrupted 1812.
Indians captured Butler sent Fort Niagara where remained until British freed parole.
returned Kentucky only join American forces that British Indians Battle Thames 1813.
During battle, Butler volunteered barn fire where enemy taken shelter.
successfully received rank colonel bravery.
Butler were sent Orleans assist Andrew Jackson city's defense.
distinguished himself during Battle Orleans.
Among under command future Florida governor Richard Keith Call, would remain lifelong friends with Butler.
1848, Butler Democratic candidate Vice President United States.
1848 Democratic National Convention, delegate Francis Blair leader movement Butler ticket with Lewis Cass, Butler nomination first ballot over John Quitman William King.
general election, ticket Cass Butler defeated Whig candidates Zachary Taylor Millard Fillmore.
interred Butler family cemetery Carrollton.
Gen. William Butler House, home Carrollton, Kentucky, listed National Register Historic Places.
